description: Jobs say they worked, but output is empty, partial, hanging, or brittle under normal pressure.
Users need clear behavior on JS-heavy pages, SPA waits, hydration, and browser-path truth.
Successful extraction still damages tables, headings, lists, code blocks, OCR layout, or document hierarchy.
Users want visible traces, queue truth, retry clarity, replay, selectors, and stronger debugging evidence.
The first demo is easy, but the first real workflow exposes setup friction or trust decay.
Users want schema-safe JSON, visible-only extraction, table-aware parsing, and richer structured outputs.
Credit shape, token spikes, and unclear recurring cost weaken buying confidence.
Users need deterministic filenames, reruns, queue state, and predictable long-job control.
Local-hosting clarity, MCP transport stability, setup completeness, and agent-readable packaging matter.
Login flows, cookies, profiles, and persisted browser state remain fragile.
Cloudflare, fingerprinting, proxies, and hostile pages push tools into failure or expensive fallback.
Users want multi-step search, map, browse, and enrichment after the first scrape works.
Some competitors show polished official surfaces but weak public complaint depth, creating uncertainty.
Signals that do not fit the main buckets cleanly.
